title: Hieracium toutonianum
taxonomy: Hieracium toutonianum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which is a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Hieracium toutonianum is further classified into the subfamily Cichorioideae and the tribe Cichorieae. The species is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.

intro: Hieracium toutonianum, also known as Touton's hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve digestion, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium toutonianum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
